Previously, only Jews, Samaritans, and converts who had been circumcised and observed the ceremonial law had been baptized into the Christian church; but now Gentiles were to be called to partake in all the privileges of God's people without first becoming Jews. Pure and undefiled religion is sometimes found where we least expect it. Wherever the fear of God rules in the heart, it will appear both in works of charity and of piety, neither should be excused from the other. Cornelius likely had true faith in God’s word as far as he understood it, though not yet a clear faith in Christ. This was the work of the Spirit of God, through the mediation of Jesus, even before Cornelius knew him, as is the case with us all when we, who before were dead in sin, are made alive. Through Christ also his prayers and alms were accepted, which otherwise would have been rejected. Without dispute or delay Cornelius was obedient to the heavenly vision. In matters concerning our souls, let us not waste time.

Peter’s prejudices against Gentiles would have prevented him from going to Cornelius unless the Lord had prepared him for this service. To tell a Jew that God had directed certain animals to be considered clean which were previously deemed unclean was essentially saying that the law of Moses was done away. Peter soon learned the meaning of it. God knows what services are before us and how to prepare us; and we know the meaning of what he has taught us when we find an occasion to use it.

When we see our calling clearly for any service, we should not be troubled by doubts and scruples arising from prejudices or former ideas. Cornelius had gathered his friends together to partake with him of the heavenly wisdom he expected from Peter. We should not desire to enjoy spiritual blessings alone. It ought to be both given and received as kindness and respect to our relatives and friends to invite them to join us in religious exercises. Cornelius declared the direction God gave him to send for Peter. We are right in our aims when we attend a gospel ministry with regard to the Divine appointment requiring us to use that ordinance. How seldom ministers are called to speak to such companies, however small, in which it may be said that they are all present in the sight of God, to hear all things that are commanded of God! But these were ready to hear what Peter was commanded of God to say.

Acceptance cannot be obtained on any other ground than that of the covenant of mercy through the atonement of Christ; but wherever true religion is found, God will accept it without regarding names or sects. The fear of God and works of righteousness are the substance of true religion, the effects of special grace. Though these are not the cause of a man’s acceptance, they show it; and whatever may be lacking in knowledge or faith will in due time be given by Him who has begun it. They knew generally the word, that is, the gospel, which God sent to the children of Israel. The purpose of this word was that God published the good news of peace through Jesus Christ. They knew the facts relating to the gospel. They knew the baptism of repentance which John preached. Let them know that this Jesus Christ, by whom peace is made between God and man, is Lord of all; not only as over all, God blessed forevermore, but as Mediator. All power, both in heaven and on earth, is put into his hand, and all judgment committed to him. God will go with those whom he anoints; he will be with those to whom he has given his Spirit. Peter then declares Christ’s resurrection from the dead, and the proofs of it. Faith has reference to a testimony, and Christian faith is built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, on the testimony they gave. See what must be believed concerning him. That we are all accountable to Christ as our Judge; so every one must seek his favor and have him as our Friend. And if we believe in him, we shall all be justified by him as our Righteousness. The remission of sins lays a foundation for all other favors and blessings by removing what hinders the bestowing of them. If sin is pardoned, all is well, and will end well forever.

The Holy Spirit fell upon others after they were baptized to confirm them in the faith; but upon these Gentiles before they were baptized to show that God does not confine himself to outward signs. The Holy Spirit fell upon those who were neither circumcised nor baptized; it is the Spirit that quickens, the flesh profits nothing. They magnified God and spoke of Christ and the benefits of redemption. Whatever gift we are endued with, we ought to honor God with it. The believing Jews who were present were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit was poured out upon the Gentiles also. By mistaken notions of things, we make it difficult to understand the methods of Divine providence and grace. As they were undeniably baptized with the Holy Ghost, Peter concluded they were not to be refused the baptism of water, and the ordinance was administered. The argument is conclusive; can we deny the sign to those who have received the things signified? Those who have some acquaintance with Christ cannot but desire more. Even those who have received the Holy Spirit must see their need of learning more of the truth daily.
